# Test stdio transport functionality

# Test mcpspy with stdio transport
exec mcpspy --pretty --disable-json-scanner -c 'echo {"jsonrpc": "2.0", "id": 1, "method": "initialize"}'

# Verify JSON-RPC structure in output
stdout '"jsonrpc": "2.0"'
stdout '"id": 1'
stdout '"method": "initialize"'

# Test timeout handling with aggressive timeout
env MCP_TOOL_TIMEOUT=2s
exec mcpspy --help
stdout 'Usage'

# Test stdin/stdout handling
cat > input.json << EOF
{"jsonrpc": "2.0", "id": 1, "method": "initialize", "params": {"protocolVersion": "2024-11-05"}}
EOF

# Test mcpspy with file input
exec mcpspy --pretty < input.json
stdout '"jsonrpc": "2.0"'
stdout '"method": "initialize"'
stdout '"protocolVersion": "2024-11-05"'

# Cleanup
exec rm input.json
